openmeter/billing/worker.purpose: Top-level Watermill-based billing worker that fans out subscription and invoice events from the system Kafka topic to typed handlers. Owns the Worker struct, WorkerOptions config, and the goroutine-safe AddHandler extension point; child packages own advance, collect, asyncadvance, and subscriptionsync logic independently.

patterns.name: WorkerOptions.Validate() constructor guard
patterns.description: All required fields are checked in Validate() before any goroutine or router is started; return error from New() if Validate() fails.
patterns.example: if err := opts.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler for each event type
patterns.description: Each Watermill event type gets its own NewGroupEventHandler closure registered in eventHandler(). The closure receives a typed event pointer and delegates to a service method.
patterns.example: grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler(func(ctx context.Context, event *billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent) error { return w.asyncAdvanceHandler.Handle(ctx, event) })

patterns.name: LockdownNamespaces guard before service call
patterns.description: Every handler closure checks slices.Contains(w.lockdownNamespaces, event.<Namespace>) and returns nil early to skip locked namespaces.
patterns.example: if event != nil && slices.Contains(w.lockdownNamespaces, event.Invoice.Namespace) { return nil }

patterns.name: Optional ChargesService nil-guard
patterns.description: ChargesService is optional; its handler is only constructed and registered when the field is non-nil. Pattern for optional sub-handlers.
patterns.example: if opts.ChargesService != nil { asyncAdvanceChargesHandler, err = chargesasyncadvance.New(...) }

patterns.name: AddHandler for external extension without recompilation
patterns.description: Post-construction callers (e.g. billing-worker cmd) use Worker.AddHandler(handler) to append handlers; handlers run after built-ins and must be idempotent.
patterns.example: worker.AddHandler(myExtraHandler) // called from app/common or cmd layer

patterns.name: Single Kafka consumer handler per worker
patterns.description: All event types flow through one router.AddConsumerHandler subscription on SystemEventsTopic; the NoPublishingHandler multiplexes by event type internally.

key_file_guides.role: Defines WorkerOptions, Worker struct, New() constructor, eventHandler() registration loop, AddHandler(), Run(), and Close(). The single authoritative place for handler registration order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new event types requires a new NewGroupEventHandler entry in eventHandler(); forgetting the lockdownNamespaces guard; making ChargesService required instead of optional breaks deployments without charges.

anti_patterns: Returning ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance from a handler closure — Watermill will nack and requeue; catch it inside the handler and return nil
Calling billing.Adapter directly from worker.go — all DB access must go through billing.Service or the injected sub-services
Adding business logic inside worker.go event closures — delegate immediately to the appropriate sub-service (asyncAdvanceHandler, subscriptionSync, etc.)
Registering a handler after New() returns without using AddHandler() — router is already built at that point
Using context.Background() inside handler closures — always use the ctx passed by Watermill via the closure parameter

decisions.decision: Single NoPublishingHandler multiplexing all event types over one Kafka subscription
decisions.rationale: Simplifies router topology; unknown event types are silently dropped, making the worker tolerant of schema evolution without redeployment.
Allows deployments where charges are disabled to run the billing worker without wiring the charges subsystem.

decisions.decision: AddHandler() extension point instead of subclassing or re-wiring
decisions.rationale: Lets app/common and cmd layer attach domain-specific handlers (e.g. reconciler) after construction without modifying the core worker package.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new subscription event type handler with lockdown guard
